title: Tetragonolobus gussonei
taxonomy: Tetragonolobus gussonei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Tetragonolobus and is native to the Mediterranean region. The specific classification of Tetragonolobus gussonei is Tetragonolobus gussonei (Guss.) Asch. & Graebn. It is a perennial herb with a scrambling habit, and its classification is often included in the tribe Fabeae. Tetragonolobus gussonei is a plant of the Cretan flora and its classification is supported by various botanical sources.
uses: Tetragonolobus gussonei, also known as the horse bean or four-angled bean, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. intro: Tetragonolobus gussonei, also known as the horse bean or horse vetch, is a leguminous plant species native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to help manage gastrointestinal issues and support cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Tetragonolobus gussonei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties.
